mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications involving large amounts of unstructured data, such as AI and machine learning workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for proper resource allocation and understanding of vector data management.

steps.title: Add the Milvus Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Milvus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

```

> This is the official Helm chart repository for Milvus.

steps.title: Install Milvus CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Milvus to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml

```

steps.title: Install Milvus using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Milvus in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --create-namespace --version 2.5.0

```

steps.title: Create a Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Milvus cluster resource to manage your vector database.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1alpha1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus-cluster
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: v2.5.0
EOF

```

> This YAML creates a basic Milvus cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1alpha1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus-cluster
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: v2.5.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Milvus cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get milvusclusters -n milvus

```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Milvus installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss unless backups are taken.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process includes draining the nodes, upgrading the Helm chart, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --version 2.5.1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get milvusclusters -n milvus

```

> Replace <node-name> with your actual node name.
> Replace <node-name> with your actual node name.

troubleshooting.title: Milvus pods are in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources allocated to the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the Milvus cluster configuration.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get milvusclusters returns 'NotFound'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the YAML definition and reapply it if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check your Kubernetes cluster status and network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Milvus service is not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service type is set to LoadBalancer or NodePort in the Helm chart values.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new vector indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old REST API endpoints in favor of the new gRPC-based API.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your application code to use the new gRPC endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus cluster listed with the command 'kubectl get milvusclusters -n milvus' showing the status as 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1alpha1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus-cluster
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: v2.5.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
